私もお客様気質すぎる、全ての絵描きはXでマネタイズしてる絵師みたいに接待しろ！って勘違い見る専さんはご遠慮願いたいんだが。絵師様のご都合で見る専は黙ってついてきて当然だろ！見返りなんてもとめるな。いいね通知くるのもうぜえみたいなpommuはさすがに、現状あのノリが居心地よい少数派の絵描きの休憩所にしかならんと思うよ。

公式アカが通知あります（ただしリプ通知のみ）でガッカリする絵描きと観る専さんお断りのβ版だからね。リプ通知のみですというのもヘルプ見ろというのも、判ってる人だけ来て欲しいβ版なのか、X不具合に乗じて大衆も大歓迎なのかどっちかわかりかねるんだよなpommu。

いいねするけど自分がいいねしたら重いかも。嫌われちゃうかもミュートされちゃうかもとか気にする方は女性向けにおおいとおもうんだよな。くるっぷとかで良く見た。その層にあわせたら、漸く通知ついたなら、Ｘ不具合あるし、pommuも試すかって人が、リプ通知だけです！引用もいいねも当然ないです！いいねなんてつけるの息苦しいからつけません！ってpommuきますかね。絵描きの休憩室であのノリが合う絵描きと休憩室におじゃましたいですって熱心なファンと、ここでのつぶやき晒したろってヲチとかしか寄りつかないのではとおもってる。DLsiteさん運営の国産ＳＮＳなので期待したんだがコレジャナイになりそうだ。

みすきーも青空もあわないからＸだけだったが。Ｘいよいよダメなのでpommu試して見るかという人もいるかもなので、一応、投稿できるのはpommuにもコピペってるけど。いまのままだとだめなんじゃないかなー。いいね通知ないのが気楽なんて褒めるのはよくある女性向けノリの国産ＳＮＳのひとつにすぎないし。女性向けの小規模国産ＳＮＳなら競合沢山なんですよね。いまさらできたところで。アカ凍結とか削除されまくった解かない限りは様子見はするが、青空やみすきーよりもＸのエロ絵描き移住に向かないとはおもってる。ＤＬのデジ同人メインの方はいいかもだけどね。

Internet → open access

SpaceX is the first to reach monopoly BEFORE regulation. The IPO makes regulation politically impossible.

images.alt:
I need to apologize. The university where I am provost used AI to replace our graduation ceremony and — to our surprise! — our cherished students were upset by the alteration. We had asked ChatGPT to devise a more efficient diploma delivery routine than the legacy procedure of walking across stage one by one at the call of the graduate’s name. The AI assistant divided our auditorium floor into a number of polyomino square sectors (it called them ‘squectors’), where students clustered according to a number of factors including height, GPA, and others, intangible. Ramps were introduced, suspended above stage and fitted to rotational hinges, so the rolled diplomas could wheel down to each squector at a controlled and geometrically appropriate rate. The AI application misparsed the auditorium floor plan and several of the squectors were drawn too small, forcing students into packed and humid clusters. At times, the rate of the diploma spool became fiercely rapid — torrential, if I’m honest — and the emblems of achievement were blown like discarded newspaper across the chamber hall, plastered finally to the floor by dirt smeared from attendees’ shoeheels in the midst of the trampling chaos.

It is the job of our institution to prepare students for the world and its changing times. While our hearts were in the right place, our missteps caused delay and unease. On behalf of the administration and our enduring institution: we will do better.

record.text: The AI stack gets silly fast: ChatGPT here, Claude there, then separate image tools on top. A more rational setup is one workspace with 13 chat models and 9 image models, starting at /month with  in credits, then paying for actual usage.

The real value of no AI cooldowns is keeping momentum. Drop in a PDF, use vision when needed, enhance the prompt, and keep moving instead of waiting for a reset. AI tools should help you finish the work, not interrupt it.

A lot of AI frustration is really model sequencing. Research with Perplexity Reasoning, think with Claude Sonnet 4.6 or GPT-5.5, then switch mid-conversation when the job changes. The right model for the next step usually beats loyalty to one default.
二人が生成した画像。右がChatGPTで、左がGemini。右は昨晩見た夜空に本当に似ている。森の手前に団地があればそっくり…。左からは特殊なサービス精神を感じてしまい。木星はニコニコ顔なのに、金星はニンマリ顔。Gemini、意外と分かってる…。今、実際のふたご座は木星の右上にあって。
5月19日。861年のこの日（新暦）に今でいう福岡県直方市に隕石が落下。直方隕石。落下の目撃記録が残っている世界最古の隕石らしく。現在も地元の神社に保存されているようで。直方市、これまで「なおかたし」だと思ってた。「のおがたし」と読むことを今日に至ってやっと知り…。
昨晩、木星・金星・三日月が斜め一直線に並んでいる夜空をうちのAIたちに生成してもらい。ここに載せたのはChatGPTが描いてくれた画像。自分が見た夜空にそっくり。Geminiはどれだけ説明しても独自の何かを付け加えようとしてくれて。星が笑っていたり、豆知識を書き込んだり。サービス精神の暴走…。

external.description: A traditional resume can only list what you’ve done. Your online presence shows how you think, build, and communicate, and that matters more now than ever. Recruiters and collaborators search your name before opening a PDF. A visible GitHub, technical articles, project walkthroughs, and consistent learning updates create trust and open doors that a CV alone cannot. This article explores why the internet has become the new resume for developers, how students and early‑career engineers can start building credibility early, and why consistency, not perfection, is what the internet rewards most. The goal isn’t internet fame; it’s professional discoverability.

external.description:
🚨

**Major Update:** This post contains a significant change that may impact your organisation.

**Introduction**

We are introducing a new organization-level setting that allows admins to migrate **Outlook on the web (OWA)** from Exchange-based **Data Loss Prevention (DLP)** policy evaluation to **Data Classification Services (DCS)** -based DLP policy evaluation. This change brings OWA in line with the new Outlook experience, enabling enhanced capabilities such as the new oversharing policy tips experience and “Wait to send.” This update helps provide a more consistent, modern, and scalable DLP experience across Outlook clients.

**When this will happen:**

  * General Availability (Worldwide): Rollout will begin **mid-May 2026** and complete by **late May 2026**.
  * General Availability (GCC): Rollout will begin **mid-May 2026** and complete by **mid-June 2026**.



**How this affects your organization:**

**Who is affected:**

  * Admins managing **Exchange Online and Microsoft Purview DLP policies**
  * Organizations using **DLP policies in Outlook on the web**



**What will happen:**

  * A new organization-level setting (`DlpViaDcsEnabled`) will be available to control DLP evaluation mode in OWA.
  * By default, the setting is `False`, meaning Exchange-based DLP remains in use.
  * Admins can manually enable DCS-based DLP evaluation for OWA using PowerShell.
  * Once enabled, OWA will use DCS-based DLP policy evaluation instead of Exchange-based evaluation.
  * Users may experience enhanced DLP features, including:
    * Improved oversharing policy tips
    * “Wait to send” functionality
    * Alignment with new Outlook DLP behavior
  * Some legacy Exchange-based DLP predicates will no longer be supported after migration. Refer to Data loss prevention policy tip reference for Outlook for Microsoft 365 | Microsoft Learn for details about supported features.



**What you can do to prepare:**

  * If you plan to continue using Exchange-based DLP evaluation, no action is required.
  * If you plan to migrate to DCS-based DLP evaluation by enabling the `DlpViaDcsEnabled` setting:
    * **Important:** Review existing DLP policies before enabling this setting. Some Exchange-based predicates are not supported with DCS-based DLP and policies using them may no longer work as expected after migration.
    * Test DCS-based DLP evaluation in a controlled environment
    * Enable the setting using Exchange Online PowerShell:
    * Communicate changes to compliance teams.
    * Update internal documentation.




    Get-OrganizationConfig | Select DlpViaDcsEnabled
    Set-OrganizationConfig -DlpViaDcsEnabled $true


**Learn more:** Set-OrganizationConfig (ExchangePowerShell) | Microsoft Learn

**Compliance considerations:**

**Compliance area**| **Explanation**
---|---
Alters how existing customer data is processed| DLP evaluation shifts from Exchange-based processing to DCS-based processing, which may change how email content is analyzed and classified. This is existing analyzation classification flows for New Outlook and Classic Outlook.
Modifies Purview Data Loss Prevention (DLP) policies or enforcement| Some Exchange-based predicates are no longer supported, and enforcement behavior may differ under DCS-based evaluation.
Includes an admin control| Admins can enable or disable the feature using the `DlpViaDcsEnabled` organization setting.
external.title: MC1315220: Outlook on the web: New setting to use data classification services for DLP evaluation

record.text: SharePoint updates simplify agent management with new Site AI settings, letting site owners control highlighted agents. Approved agents won’t appear in the Agent Picker and should be placed on pages. Rollout starts mid-November 2024, completing by June 2026, affecting all SharePoint Online tenants.

external.description:
**[Introduction]**

We’re **updating how agents in SharePoint work** to provide a simpler experience for agents selected on a site.

As a refresher, Get started with agents in SharePoint - Microsoft Support, there are two types of agents in SharePoint:

  * Ready-made agent - Every SharePoint site comes with a "ready-made agent", automatically scoped to the content on that site. These agents, scoped to the SharePoint site, require no building from site admins or site owners.
  * Custom-built agent - With site editing permissions, you can create custom agents with a specific content scope, identity, and behavior.



With this update, we're introducing a **new Site AI settings** to give site owners more control over which agents are highlighted on their sites. Approved agents will no longer appear in the Agent Picker, so these agents should be placed directly on the site page for better discoverability.

**[When this will happen]**

**General Availability (Worldwide):** We began rolling out in __**mid-November 2024** and expect rollout to complete by** _early June 2026_**.

**[How this affects your organization]**

_Who is affected_

  * All Microsoft 365 tenants using SharePoint Online
  * Site owners and content authors managing how custom agents are discovered on their site



 _What will happen_

  * Site owners will manage the site’s "Default" agent through the main agent setting in the new **Site AI settings** panel in _SharePoint site settings_.
  * For existing sites, the current "Default" agent will automatically become the site’s main agent.
  * For new sites, the main agent will be the ready-made agent
  * Users no longer access approved agents through the agent picker and overflow menu in the _chat pane_. All approved agents remain in the _Site Assets\Copilots\Approved_ folder.
  * To help users discover agents, site owners and builders should surface agents directly on pages using supported web parts or shared agent links.



This feature is **on by default** and does not require tenant-level configuration changes.

**[What you can do to prepare]**

No immediate admin action is required. We recommend the following preparation steps:

  * Ask site owners and builders to review the approved agents on their sites in the _Site Assets\Copilots\Approved_ folder.
  * Encourage site owners to add or update agent links on pages using supported web parts, such as: Agent Link web part, Editorial Card web part, and adding shared .agent file links in Text web part
  * Inform users that agents they **recently used** can be found in:
    * _Build “Owned by me” section by choosing the Agents filter,_
    * _OneDrive_ web **_Home > Recent_** view (filterable by agent), or
    *  _Microsoft 365 Copilot agents list_



**Learn more:**

  * Use the Agent Link web part | Microsoft Support
  * Use the Editorial Card web part | Microsoft Support
  * Add text, tables, and images to your page with the Text web part | Microsoft Support



**[Compliance considerations]**

No compliance considerations identified. Review as appropriate for your organization.
external.title: MC1315219: Update to agents in SharePoint: Simpler launch experience and new site AI settings

external.description:
We are announcing the ability for Customer service representatives (CSRs) to configure a secondary audio device for their sound setting personalization on the Digital Messaging channels in Dynamics 365 Contact Center. This feature will be available in public preview on May 22, 2026.

**How does this affect me?**
Customer service representatives (CSRs) can set up a secondary audio device to help ensure they don’t miss assigned conversations on digital messaging channels.

  * **Notification type control:** CSRs can configure secondary device sound settings at the notification type level, including incoming conversation alerts and new message alerts.
  * **Channel-specific settings:** Settings can be applied at the individual digital messaging channel level, giving each representative control over their audio notification experience.
  * **User-level personalization:** Each CSR manages their own secondary device preferences with no impact on other representatives or existing channel configurations.



**What do I need to do to prepare?**
This message is for awareness, and no action is required.
external.title: MC1315214: Dynamics 365 Contact Center – Secondary audio device for notifications in Digital Messaging conversations

record.text: Launched: Microsoft 365 Copilot Adoption Hub Redesign

external.description: One of the biggest barriers to Copilot adoption is people don’t always know where to start. Today, we released the first version of our redesigned Microsoft 365 Copilot business user hub. Take a look. We set out to simplify adoption by making the experience more practical and focused on key roles: AI Business User, AI Champion and AI Leader. Microsoft 365 Copilot adoption hub What you’ll find: Prompts you can apply immediately in your work. • Real examples of how Copilot helps across tasks. • Clear guidance based on your role. What's the same: Connection to what’s new, communities and event information. Advanced guidance for User Enablement and IT Professionals. Content from Microsoft Learn to advance your skilling through the AI Skills Navigator and learning paths for certification. Your feedback was essential in crafting this evolution of how to learn and use AI experiences from Microsoft. Keep sharing your insights via our feedback form at aka.ms/amc/feedback. The entire team reads what you submit and innovates to support your needs. That’s really the focus here; helping people get started and keep going to get work done. We’d love your input! What do you think, and what content would you like to see us build next? #Copilot #AIAdoption
